maxContractTerm: **Five (5) years for a single award term, with one (1) statutorily authorized renewal of up to an additional five (5) years (total potential exposure: 10 years across an initial award and one renewal). A discrete federal-funding exception allows contracts up to ten (10) years (single term) if they receive funding under the federal Clean School Bus Program, 42 U.S.C. § 16091.** Idaho Code § 33-1510(2) provides verbatim: "No contract shall be executed covering a period of time exceeding five (5) years. School districts shall advertise, bid and contract for all bus transportation service routes at a single time, and contract with the lowest responsible bidder or bidders meeting the specifications; provided that, one (1) time only, a school district may renew a contract with the current contractor if the board of trustees, after renegotiation with the contractor, determines that the terms are satisfactory to the district. The board of trustees may renew the contract for a term not to exceed five (5) years." Idaho Code § 33-1510(4) provides: "Notwithstanding the provisions of subsection (2) of this section and any provision of law or rule to the contrary, contracts that receive federal funding pursuant to the federal clean school bus program, 42 U.S.C. 16091, may exceed five (5) years but may not exceed ten (10) years." The State Department of Education's Model Contract for Transportation Services implements this verbatim: "This Contract shall commence on the Effective Date and expire on [date] for a total period of [xx - not to exceed five (5)] (xx) year(s), unless sooner terminated in accordance with the provisions of this Contract. The parties may renew this Contract one (1) time for a period not to exceed five (5) years upon a determination by the Board of Trustees of the District, after renegotiation with the Contractor, that the terms of the renewal are satisfactory to the District." The statute does not authorize annual-termination-permitted multi-year terms; the model contract expressly forecloses termination for convenience ("Nothing in this section shall be construed as ability by the District to terminate for its convenience"), though contracts remain subject to a "Termination for Fiscal Necessity" clause tied to legislative appropriation and board budget approval. Citation: Idaho Code § 33-1510(2) & (4), https://legislature.idaho.gov/statutesrules/idstat/Title33/T33CH15/SECT33-1510/.
rebidRequired: **Required at the end of the initial awarded term unless the district exercises the single, statutorily authorized renewal with the incumbent contractor.** Idaho Code § 33-1510(2) mandates that "School districts shall advertise, bid and contract for all bus transportation service routes at a single time, and contract with the lowest responsible bidder or bidders meeting the specifications." Idaho Code § 33-1510(3) reinforces: "Before entering into such contracts, the board of trustees shall invite bids by twice giving notice as provided in section 33-402(2), Idaho Code, and shall award the contract to the lowest responsible bidder." Bidding notice must be published "in a newspaper of general circulation as required by chapter 1, title 60, Idaho Code, except that the notice for contracting for transportation services shall be made not less than four (4) weeks before the date of opening bids" (Idaho Code § 33-402(2)). At term-end, the default rule is a competitive sealed-bid rebid under § 33-1510(2)-(3); the only statutory exit from rebid is the single renewal option in § 33-1510(2), which itself is conditional: "Renewal of any contract pursuant to this section shall not be granted unless the provisions of this section were included, in a substantially conforming summary, within the bidding notice, published pursuant to section 33-601, Idaho Code, of the contract." In other words, if the renewal possibility was not disclosed in the original bid notice, the district cannot renew and must rebid. After the one renewal (if exercised) expires, the district must run a new competitive bidding cycle. The general procurement statute (Idaho Code § 67-2806, applicable to political subdivisions including school districts via Idaho Code § 33-601(2)) is supplementary: "Any general procurement procedures set forth in this chapter shall be supplemented by the provisions of any specific statute pertaining to the awarding of any contract for the purchase or acquisition of any service, commodity or thing made expressly applicable to any particular political subdivision" (Idaho Code § 67-2802). The transportation-specific § 33-1510 therefore controls over the general rules; nothing in Idaho Code § 67-2803 (general procurement exclusions) categorically exempts pupil transportation from § 33-1510's bidding mandate. Citation: Idaho Code §§ 33-1510(2)-(3), 33-402(2), https://legislature.idaho.gov/statutesrules/idstat/Title33/T33CH15/SECT33-1510/.
renewalMechanics: **One (1) board-approved renewal, by mutual written agreement after renegotiation, with the incumbent contractor, for a term up to five (5) years - but only if the renewal option was disclosed in a "substantially conforming summary" in the original bidding notice.** Idaho Code § 33-1510(2) sets out the full mechanism: "one (1) time only, a school district may renew a contract with the current contractor if the board of trustees, after renegotiation with the contractor, determines that the terms are satisfactory to the district. The board of trustees may renew the contract for a term not to exceed five (5) years. Renewal of any contract pursuant to this section shall not be granted unless the provisions of this section were included, in a substantially conforming summary, within the bidding notice, published pursuant to section 33-601, Idaho Code, of the contract." Mechanical elements established by statute and SDE-approved model contract: (1) **One renewal only** - no statutory authority for a second renewal; after one renewal expires the district must rebid. (2) **Renegotiation required** - renewal is not automatic; the board and contractor must renegotiate and the board must affirmatively determine the renewal terms are satisfactory. (3) **Board approval required** - the board of trustees, not just administration, must approve. (4) **Disclosure-in-bid-notice prerequisite** - failure to summarize the renewal authority in the original notice forecloses any renewal. (5) **State superintendent approval of the contract instrument** - under § 33-1510(1), the executed contract (including any renewal) must be submitted to the state superintendent of public instruction for review of legal requirements and appropriate costs before both parties sign; the superintendent has 21 calendar days to approve or recommend/require changes, with appeal to the State Board of Education. (6) **CPI / fuel escalators are permitted (not required) within the contract** - the SDE Model Contract for Transportation Services states verbatim: "Price escalator clauses may be included but must be proportional to an objectively determined index, such as the Consumer Price Index. Fuel escalator clauses may also be used at the discretion of the parties. However, the combination of a general price escalator term and a fuel escalator clause must be drafted carefully to protect against double compensation of the Contractor." (7) **No statutory automatic/evergreen rollover** - the statute requires affirmative board action after renegotiation; silence does not produce a renewal. (8) **No agency-approval requirement beyond the state-superintendent contract review** - the State Board of Education is involved only on appeal of a superintendent decision under § 33-1510(1). Citation: Idaho Code § 33-1510(1)-(2), https://legislature.idaho.gov/statutesrules/idstat/Title33/T33CH15/SECT33-1510/; SDE Model Contract for Transportation Services (June 2025), https://www.sde.idaho.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/06/Model-Contract-for-Transportation-Services.docx.
statutoryCitation: **Primary (transportation-specific):** Idaho Code § 33-1510 - "Contracts for Transportation Service" (Title 33 [Education], Chapter 15 [Transportation of Pupils]). Last amended by 2022 Idaho Sess. Laws ch. 87, § 1, p. 251 (adding the federal Clean School Bus Program 10-year exception in subsection (4)). Originally enacted by 1963 ch. 13, § 88. Not amended in 2023, 2024, 2025, or 2026 regular sessions. Official text: https://legislature.idaho.gov/statutesrules/idstat/Title33/T33CH15/SECT33-1510/.  **Supporting transportation provision:** Idaho Code § 33-402(2) - Notice Requirements (publication "not less than four (4) weeks before the date of opening bids" for transportation services). Last amended 2011 ch. 151, § 15. https://legislature.idaho.gov/statutesrules/idstat/title33/t33ch4/sect33-402/.  **General school-district contracting cross-reference:** Idaho Code § 33-601(2) directs that school district contracts "shall be executed in accordance with the provisions of chapter 28, title 67, Idaho Code" except for curricular materials. Last amended 2012 ch. 15, § 1. https://legislature.idaho.gov/statutesrules/idstat/title33/t33ch6/sect33-601/.  **General procurement (supplementary to § 33-1510):** Idaho Code Title 67, Chapter 28 - Purchasing by Political Subdivisions - particularly §§ 67-2801 (legislative intent), 67-2802 (applicability: "Any general procurement procedures set forth in this chapter shall be supplemented by the provisions of any specific statute pertaining to the awarding of any contract … made expressly applicable to any particular political subdivision"), 67-2803 (exclusions; last amended 2025 ch. 133, § 1), 67-2806 (procuring services or personal property - $100,000/$250,000 thresholds for sealed bidding; last amended 2025 ch. 133, § 3). https://legislature.idaho.gov/statutesrules/idstat/Title67/T67CH28/SECT67-2802/.  **State Board of Education authority:** Idaho Code § 33-1511 (powers and duties related to transportation; last amended 2004 ch. 135). https://legislature.idaho.gov/statutesrules/idstat/Title33/T33CH15/SECT33-1511/.  **State-agency model contract (mandatory by force of § 33-1510(1)):** SDE Model Contract for Transportation Services (June 2025 version). https://www.sde.idaho.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/06/Model-Contract-for-Transportation-Services.docx.  **SDE Student Transportation departmental page (current portal):** https://www.sde.idaho.gov/about-us/departments/student-transportation/.  **Administrative code (operational only - no procurement carve-out):** IDAPA 08.02.02 (Rules Governing Uniformity), § 150 (Transportation), § 160 (Maintenance Standards and Inspections), § 170 (School Bus Drivers and Vehicle Operation), § 180 (Written Policy), § 190 (Program Operations). https://adminrules.idaho.gov/rules/current/08/080202.pdf.
districtVariance: **No** - within the substantive boundaries set by § 33-1510. The statute mandates use of "the current pupil transportation model contract developed by the state department of education" for all districts ("All contracts entered into by boards of trustees for the transportation of pupils shall be in writing using the current pupil transportation model contract developed by the state department of education." Idaho Code § 33-1510(1)), and every executed contract must be submitted to the state superintendent of public instruction for review and approval within 21 calendar days before signing. This produces uniform statewide application of the 5-year cap, single-renewal rule, sealed-bid requirement, and bidding-notice timing. Districts may attach addenda for local requirements (route specifications, payment schedules, escalator clauses, performance bonds), and the model contract itself leaves several parameters (e.g., exact term within the 5-year ceiling, choice and parameters of CPI or fuel escalator, payment-schedule mechanics) to the parties - so practical contract content varies, but the statutory rules on term, rebid, and renewal mechanics apply uniformly. Sample district contracts available on the public web (e.g., Mountain Home SD No. 193 - Caldwell Transportation Co. SY24-29 contract published at https://www.mtnhomesd.org/) and reporting on prior award cycles each recite the § 33-1510(2) framework and operate within the 5-year/single-renewal structure, with no evidence of substantive district-level divergence on the contract-term, rebid, or renewal-mechanics rules.
specialEducation: **No separate term-limit, rebid, procurement, or renewal regime exists for special-education or other categorically protected transportation under Idaho state law.** Idaho Code § 33-1510 governs "All contracts entered into by boards of trustees for the transportation of pupils" without carve-out for special-education, IEP-mandated, McKinney-Vento, or foster-care transportation. The SDE "Transportation of Students with Disabilities" guidance (REVIEWED December 2024, https://www.sde.idaho.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/Transportation-of-Students-with-Disabilities.pdf) addresses operational, IEP-team, FAPE/LRE, and behavioral-management issues for special-ed transportation, but does not establish any distinct contracting/procurement framework, contract-term cap, renewal mechanism, or bidding exemption - it operates entirely within the operational/related-services framework of IDEA and the Idaho Special Education Manual (sections on District Responsibility, IEP Team Decision, Transportation Representative as IEP Team Member, Determining Special Transportation Needs, Managing Behavior on the Bus, and Resources). Federal IDEA (20 U.S.C. § 1401 et seq.) is relevant only to the substantive duty to provide special transportation as a related service when required by the IEP; it does not displace or modify the state procurement/contract rules in § 33-1510. The 2025 amendments by ch. 278 to chapter 15 (H 396) expanded "authorized vehicles" provisions including for "preschool children, special needs children, homeless children, foster children, children inaccessible to school buses, and students placed in alternative schools or for work programs or vocational or trades training" - but this is an operational/equipment-eligibility rule for what vehicles may carry these students, not a separate procurement framework. There is no Idaho administrative-code provision (IDAPA 08.02.02 governs operations: maintenance/inspection, drivers, written-policy requirements) nor any State Board of Education rule that creates a distinct special-education transportation contracting regime. Conclusion: Idaho applies the § 33-1510 5-year/single-renewal/sealed-bid framework uniformly to regular-route and special-education/IEP/McKinney-Vento/foster transportation contracts with private contractors.
notesAndSpecialProvisions:
- **State-superintendent contract-approval gateway (Idaho Code § 33-1510(1)).** Every executed pupil transportation contract - including renewals and addenda - must be submitted to the state superintendent of public instruction *before* both parties sign for "a review of legal requirements and appropriate costs and for final approval." The superintendent has 21 calendar days from postmarked receipt to approve, recommend changes, or require changes; districts may appeal required changes to the State Board of Education, which may approve the contract without such changes.

- **Mandatory use of the SDE Model Contract (Idaho Code § 33-1510(1)).** "All contracts entered into by boards of trustees for the transportation of pupils shall be in writing using the current pupil transportation model contract developed by the state department of education." Current model version: "Model Contract for Transportation Services" available at https://www.sde.idaho.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/06/Model-Contract-for-Transportation-Services.docx.

- **Federal Clean School Bus Program 10-year extension (Idaho Code § 33-1510(4)).** Added by 2022 ch. 87. Contracts receiving funding under the federal Clean School Bus Program (42 U.S.C. § 16091) may exceed five years up to a maximum of ten (10) years - a discrete and conditional exception to the general five-year cap.

- **Single-time renewal contingent on bid-notice disclosure.** The one-time, up-to-five-year renewal in § 33-1510(2) "shall not be granted unless the provisions of this section were included, in a substantially conforming summary, within the bidding notice, published pursuant to section 33-601, Idaho Code, of the contract." Failure to summarize the renewal authority in the original public notice forecloses the renewal - the district must then rebid.

- **All routes bid at a single time (Idaho Code § 33-1510(2)).** "School districts shall advertise, bid and contract for all bus transportation service routes at a single time, and contract with the lowest responsible bidder or bidders meeting the specifications." This prevents piecemeal route-by-route procurement.

- **Notice timing (Idaho Code § 33-402(2)).** Bidding notice must be published twice in a newspaper of general circulation, and "the notice for contracting for transportation services shall be made not less than four (4) weeks before the date of opening bids."

- **Lowest-responsible-bidder rule (Idaho Code § 33-1510(3)).** "Before entering into such contracts, the board of trustees shall invite bids by twice giving notice as provided in section 33-402(2), Idaho Code, and shall award the contract to the lowest responsible bidder."

- **CPI / fuel escalator clauses (SDE Model Contract, verbatim).** Permitted but not required: "Price escalator clauses may be included but must be proportional to an objectively determined index, such as the Consumer Price Index. Fuel escalator clauses may also be used at the discretion of the parties. However, the combination of a general price escalator term and a fuel escalator clause must be drafted carefully to protect against double compensation of the Contractor."

- **Termination for Fiscal Necessity (SDE Model Contract).** All contracts are subject to legislative appropriation, federal funding, and annual local-budget approval; the district may terminate in whole or in part within ten (10) calendar days' notice if funds are not budgeted or otherwise available. **However, the model contract expressly forecloses termination for convenience**: "Nothing in this section shall be construed as ability by the District to terminate for its convenience."

- **Termination for Cause (SDE Model Contract).** Standard default triggers - contractor insolvency, bankruptcy filing, route abandonment, refusal to proceed, schedule failure, assignment for benefit of creditors, and other defaults; the district may employ another contractor and hold the original contractor responsible for added expense or damages.

- **Title 67 ch. 28 (Purchasing by Political Subdivisions) is supplementary, not controlling.** Idaho Code § 33-601(2) directs school district contracts to be executed under Title 67 ch. 28, and § 67-2802 expressly provides: "Any general procurement procedures set forth in this chapter shall be supplemented by the provisions of any specific statute pertaining to the awarding of any contract for the purchase or acquisition of any service, commodity or thing made expressly applicable to any particular political subdivision." The transportation-specific § 33-1510 therefore controls the term cap, renewal mechanics, and bidding procedure; Title 67 ch. 28 (e.g., the $100,000/$250,000 service-procurement thresholds in § 67-2806, as amended by 2025 ch. 133, § 3) supplements as a default for matters § 33-1510 does not address.

- **No statutory small-purchase, sole-source, emergency, or piggyback/cooperative-purchasing exemption from § 33-1510 specifically for pupil transportation.** Idaho Code § 67-2803 (Title 67 ch. 28 exclusions, last amended 2025 ch. 133, § 1) lists eighteen categories exempt from general political-subdivision procurement (e.g., real-property interests, insurance, JPAs, GSA schedules, software maintenance of existing systems) - but none of these categorically exempt pupil-transportation services, and § 33-1510's transportation-specific advertising/bidding mandate is not displaced by these general exclusions. The closest related general exclusion (§ 67-2803(1)) permits a political subdivision to "duplicate the price and substance" of another competitively bid contract - practical reach for pupil transportation is uncertain and would, in any event, need to respect § 33-1510's transportation-specific bidding mandate.

- **No charter-school carve-out.** Idaho Code § 33-1510 applies to "boards of trustees" - i.e., the boards of public school districts and specially chartered school districts (see § 33-1501). Public charter schools are separately governed; their transportation contracting practices, if any, are not addressed by § 33-1510.

- **Special-education transportation is governed by the same § 33-1510 framework.** SDE Special Education guidance (REVIEWED December 2024) addresses IEP-team decisions, FAPE/LRE, behavior, and discipline - no separate procurement/term/renewal regime for special-education transportation contracts under Idaho state law.

- **No statutory cap on number of board-approved escalator adjustments within a contract term**, but escalator language must be drafted to avoid double-compensation (per SDE Model Contract guidance) and must be objectively indexed.

- **Bus-driver / vehicle-inspection / insurance requirements (§§ 33-1505 through 33-1509, 33-1515; IDAPA 08.02.02) apply to both district-operated and contractor-operated services** - these are operational standards, not procurement rules.

- **2025 ch. 278 (H 396) amendments** modernized §§ 33-1501, 33-1504, 33-1506, 33-1509, and 33-1515 (authorized-vehicle types and inspection regime) but did not amend § 33-1510. **2026 H 815** amended only § 33-1006 (Transportation Support Program funding) and did not touch § 33-1510. § 33-1510 has not been amended since 2022 ch. 87.
exceptionsUnresolved: All five fields resolved on primary authority. No unresolved field after adversarial validation. Negative search paths documented for: any non-Clean-School-Bus longer-term authority (none found); any non-renewal procurement exit from rebid at term-end (none found beyond the conditional § 33-1510(2) single renewal); any administrative-code provision modifying term/rebid/renewal (none found - IDAPA 08.02.02 is operational only); any separate special-education procurement regime (none found); any automatic/evergreen renewal authority (none found).

sourceLedgerRows.citation: Idaho Code § 33-1510 (Contracts for Transportation Service), as last amended by 2022 Idaho Sess. Laws ch. 87, § 1, p. 251.
sourceLedgerRows.codeCurrency: Idaho Statutes are updated to the website July 1 following each legislative session. § 33-1510 not amended in 2023, 2024, 2025, or 2026 regular sessions (verified via Code Sections Affected indices and direct review of 2025 H 396 / Ch. 278 and 2026 H 815). Current as of 2026-06-23.
sourceLedgerRows.fieldsSupported: Max Contract Term, Rebid Required, Renewal Mechanics, Statutory Citation, Special Education (uniform application)
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: (1) All contracts entered into by boards of trustees for the transportation of pupils shall be in writing using the current pupil transportation model contract developed by the state department of education. ... (2) No contract shall be executed covering a period of time exceeding five (5) years. School districts shall advertise, bid and contract for all bus transportation service routes at a single time, and contract with the lowest responsible bidder or bidders meeting the specifications; provided that, one (1) time only, a school district may renew a contract with the current contractor if the board of trustees, after renegotiation with the contractor, determines that the terms are satisfactory to the district. The board of trustees may renew the contract for a term not to exceed five (5) years. Renewal of any contract pursuant to this section shall not be granted unless the provisions of this section were included, in a substantially conforming summary, within the bidding notice, published pursuant to section 33-601, Idaho Code, of the contract. (3) Before entering into such contracts, the board of trustees shall invite bids by twice giving notice as provided in section 33-402(2), Idaho Code, and shall award the contract to the lowest responsible bidder. (4) Notwithstanding the provisions of subsection (2) of this section and any provision of law or rule to the contrary, contracts that receive federal funding pursuant to the federal clean school bus program, 42 U.S.C. 16091, may exceed five (5) years but may not exceed ten (10) years.

sourceLedgerRows.citation: Idaho Code § 33-402 (Notice Requirements), Title 33 ch. 4. Last amended 2011 ch. 151, § 15.
sourceLedgerRows.codeCurrency: Current as of 2026-06-23. No amendments since 2011 affecting subsection (2).
sourceLedgerRows.fieldsSupported: Rebid Required, Renewal Mechanics (bidding-notice timing for transportation services)
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: (2) Notices calling for bids for the acquisition, use, or disposal of real and personal property as provided for in section 33-601, Idaho Code, and contracting for transportation services as provided for in section 33-1510, Idaho Code, shall be given in a newspaper of general circulation as required by chapter 1, title 60, Idaho Code, except that the notice for contracting for transportation services shall be made not less than four (4) weeks before the date of opening bids.

sourceLedgerRows.sourceTier: 1 - Codified state statute (general school-district contracting cross-reference)

sourceLedgerRows.fieldsSupported: Rebid Required, Renewal Mechanics (channels school-district contracts to Title 67 ch. 28 generally, while § 33-1510 controls transportation-specifically)
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: (2) To contract for the construction, repair, or improvement of any real property, or the acquisition, purchase or repair of any equipment, or other personal property necessary for the operation of the school district. Except for the purchase of curricular materials as defined in section 33-118A, Idaho Code, such contract shall be executed in accordance with the provisions of chapter 28, title 67, Idaho Code.

sourceLedgerRows.citation: Idaho Code § 67-2802 (Applicability), Title 67 ch. 28 - Purchasing by Political Subdivisions.
sourceLedgerRows.fieldsSupported: Rebid Required, Renewal Mechanics, District Variance (confirms § 33-1510 controls as transportation-specific statute; general procurement procedures are supplementary only)
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: The provisions of this chapter establish procurement requirements for all political subdivisions of the state of Idaho. ... Any general procurement procedures set forth in this chapter shall be supplemented by the provisions of any specific statute pertaining to the awarding of any contract for the purchase or acquisition of any service, commodity or thing made expressly applicable to any particular political subdivision or by means of any additional administrative process that otherwise establishes additional express requirements.

sourceLedgerRows.citation: Idaho Code § 67-2803 (Exclusions), Title 67 ch. 28. Last amended 2025 ch. 133, § 1.
sourceLedgerRows.codeCurrency: Last amended 2025 ch. 133, § 1. Current as of 2026-06-23.
sourceLedgerRows.fieldsSupported: Rebid Required, Notes (negative finding: pupil transportation NOT in the eighteen enumerated exclusions; piggyback exclusion § 67-2803(1) does not displace § 33-1510's transportation-specific bidding mandate)
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: Eighteen-category exclusion list including: contracts that duplicate the price and substance of contracts competitively bid by other public agencies; construction or service/goods under $100,000; employee wages; independent-contractor services; real-property interests; insurance; JPAs; used personal property; GSA/MAS federal schedules; state-purchasing-division contracts; goods for direct resale; travel/training; Idaho correctional industries products; heavy equipment repair; existing software maintenance/licenses; public utilities; food for jails/detention; used equipment at auctions. Pupil transportation is NOT enumerated.

sourceLedgerRows.citation: Idaho Code § 67-2806 (Procuring Services or Personal Property), Title 67 ch. 28. Last amended 2025 ch. 133, § 3.
sourceLedgerRows.codeCurrency: Last amended 2025 ch. 133, § 3 (prior 2023 ch. 165; 2017 ch. 197). Current as of 2026-06-23.
sourceLedgerRows.fieldsSupported: Notes (general $100,000-$250,000 written-solicitation threshold; over-$250,000 sealed-bid threshold - supplementary to § 33-1510)
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: $100,000-$250,000: solicitation for bids supplied to no fewer than three (3) vendors by written means. Over $250,000: open competitive sealed bid process to the lowest responsible bidder.

sourceLedgerRows.citation: Idaho Code § 33-1511 (State Board of Education - Powers and Duties Related to Transportation). Last amended 2004 ch. 135.

sourceLedgerRows.fieldsSupported: Notes (state-board oversight of pupil-transportation programs, audits, withholding of reimbursement)
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: Confers powers on the State Board of Education including designating a supervisor of school transportation, adopting minimum bus-construction standards, authorizing program reviews and fiscal audits, requiring corrective-action progress reports, and withholding pupil-transportation reimbursement funding for noncompliance - with appeal to the state board.

sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: Term and Renewal. This Contract shall commence on the Effective Date and expire on [date] for a total period of [xx - not to exceed five (5)] (xx) year(s), unless sooner terminated in accordance with the provisions of this Contract. The parties may renew this Contract one (1) time for a period not to exceed five (5) years upon a determination by the Board of Trustees of the District, after renegotiation with the Contractor, that the terms of the renewal are satisfactory to the District. ... Payment Schedule. [A payment clause such as the following must be included, but the exact terms may be determined by the parties. ... Price escalator clauses may be included but must be proportional to an objectively determined index, such as the Consumer Price Index. Fuel escalator clauses may also be used at the discretion of the parties. However, the combination of a general price escalator term and a fuel escalator clause must be drafted carefully to protect against double compensation of the Contractor.] ... Termination for Fiscal Necessity. ... Nothing in this section shall be construed as ability by the District to terminate for its convenience.

Entirely operational. No procurement, contracting, term-limit, or renewal language differentiating special-education transportation from regular-route transportation under state law.

sourceLedgerRows.citation: IDAPA 08.02.02 (State Board of Education - Rules Governing Uniformity), including § 150 (Transportation), § 160 (Maintenance Standards and Inspections), § 170 (School Bus Drivers and Vehicle Operation), § 180 (Written Policy), § 190 (Program Operations).
sourceLedgerRows.codeCurrency: Current edition; effective March 15, 2022 codification still controlling. Full PDF text reviewed 2026-06-23.
sourceLedgerRows.fieldsSupported: Rebid Required, Renewal Mechanics (negative - administrative rule addresses operational standards, not contract procurement, term, or renewal)
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: § 150 TRANSPORTATION: Minimum School Bus Construction Standards (cross-references § 33-1511). § 160 MAINTENANCE STANDARDS AND INSPECTIONS: bus safety and operating condition. § 170 SCHOOL BUS DRIVERS AND VEHICLE OPERATION: training/performance/operation per Standards for Idaho School Buses. § 180 WRITTEN POLICY: board of trustees will establish written policies governing the pupil transportation system; districts providing activity bus transportation shall have comprehensive policies. § 190 PROGRAM OPERATIONS: cross-references Pupil Transportation Support Program standards including 'contracting for transportation services' as a reimbursement-eligibility category. No section establishes or varies contract term limits, rebid requirements, or renewal mechanics for transportation services - these remain governed exclusively by Idaho Code § 33-1510 and § 33-402(2).

sourceLedgerRows.sourceTier: 1 - Session law check (negative finding for 2023-2026 amendments to § 33-1510)
sourceLedgerRows.citation: Idaho 2025 ch. 278 (H 396) amended §§ 33-1501, 33-1504, 33-1506, 33-1509, 33-1515 (authorized-vehicle definitions and inspection regime) - no amendment to § 33-1510. Idaho 2026 H 815 amended only § 33-1006 (Transportation Support Program funding) - no amendment to § 33-1510.
sourceLedgerRows.codeCurrency: 2023, 2024, 2025, and 2026 enacted-legislation lists and Code Sections Affected indices reviewed; verified by direct review of 2025 H 396 and 2026 H 815 bill text. Accessed 2026-06-23.
sourceLedgerRows.fieldsSupported: Statutory Citation (confirms § 33-1510 unchanged since 2022 ch. 87 amendment)
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: No 2023, 2024, 2025, or 2026 enacted Idaho legislation has amended Idaho Code § 33-1510. The most recent amendment was 2022 ch. 87, § 1, p. 251, which added the federal Clean School Bus Program 10-year exception now codified at § 33-1510(4).

challengeMatrixRows.findingChallenged: Max Contract Term = 5 years initial + 1 renewal of up to 5 years; Clean School Bus federal-funded exception up to 10-year single term
challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: There is some non-Clean-School-Bus longer-term, grandfathering, or 'notwithstanding/multi-year/successive/option/extension' authority in Title 33 ch. 15, Title 67 ch. 28, IDAPA 08.02.02, or official guidance that authorizes contracts longer than 5 years for routine pupil transportation, OR an annual-termination multi-year arrangement.
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: Direct re-fetch of Idaho Code § 33-1510 official page; direct re-fetch of all Title 67 ch. 28 sections (§§ 67-2801, 2802, 2803, 2806); IDAPA 08.02.02 full PDF (40 pages, all sections .150-.190 reviewed); search 'Idaho 33-1510 amended 2023 OR 2024 OR 2025 OR 2026'; 2025 H 396 / Ch. 278 bill text review; 2026 H 815 bill text review; search 'Idaho school bus contract annual termination evergreen automatic renewal'.
challengeMatrixRows.result: Confirmed. § 33-1510(2)'s 5-year cap is exclusive except for the § 33-1510(4) federal Clean School Bus 10-year exception. No grandfathering, multi-year, successive, option, or annual-termination authority anywhere in the statutes, admin code, or official guidance. § 33-1510 has not been amended since 2022 ch. 87.

challengeMatrixRows.findingChallenged: Rebid Required at term-end (unless single renewal exercised)
challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: There exists a sole-source, emergency-procurement, piggyback/cooperative-purchasing, small-purchase, interlocal, or state-agency-approval exception that lets a district extend beyond term without rebid (other than the conditional § 33-1510(2) one-time renewal).
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: Direct re-fetch of § 67-2803 exclusions list (all 18 enumerated categories reviewed); § 67-2806 thresholds reviewed; search 'Idaho pupil transportation sole source emergency procurement piggyback cooperative'; review § 33-1510(2)-(3) for exit pathways; IDAPA 08.02.02 § 190 Program Operations check.
challengeMatrixRows.result: Confirmed. § 67-2803 lists 18 exclusions; pupil transportation is NOT among them. § 67-2803(1) piggyback exclusion does not displace § 33-1510's transportation-specific bidding mandate (§ 67-2802 expressly subordinates general procurement to specific-statute provisions). Sole § 33-1510 exit at term-end is the conditional one-time renewal under § 33-1510(2).

challengeMatrixRows.findingChallenged: Renewal Mechanics: 1 board-approved renewal of up to 5 years after renegotiation, subject to disclosure-in-bid-notice prerequisite, state-superintendent approval, no automatic/evergreen rollover
challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: Statute or admin code or model contract somewhere authorizes a SECOND renewal, an automatic/evergreen rollover, CPI/fuel cap inconsistent with Stage 1 description, or a different agency-approval pathway.
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: Re-fetch full § 33-1510 verbatim; extract verbatim text of SDE Model Contract from .docx XML (full 92 paragraphs); IDAPA 08.02.02 full PDF; search 'Idaho school bus contract evergreen automatic renewal second renewal'.
challengeMatrixRows.result: Confirmed. Verbatim text of § 33-1510(2) limits renewal to 'one (1) time only.' SDE Model Contract verbatim limits renewal to 'one (1) time' for 'a period not to exceed five (5) years' upon board determination after renegotiation. Escalator language verbatim matches Stage 1 quotation. No second-renewal, automatic, or evergreen authority anywhere. State Board of Education involvement is only on appeal of a state-superintendent contract-review decision (§ 33-1510(1)).

challengeMatrixRows.findingChallenged: Statutory Citation - Idaho Code § 33-1510 controls; Title 67 ch. 28 supplements only
challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: The cited statute does not exist, is renumbered, repealed, or has been superseded; OR Title 67 ch. 28 (general procurement) overrides § 33-1510 (transportation-specific) for some category.
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: Direct re-fetch of § 33-1510 (Title 33 ch. 15) official page; direct re-fetch of § 67-2802 official page (subordination language); 2023-2026 Code Sections Affected indices; 2025 H 396 and 2026 H 815 bill text.
challengeMatrixRows.result: Confirmed. § 33-1510 fetched verbatim from official Idaho Legislature page; chapter index lists it as Section 10 of Chapter 15. § 67-2802 verbatim text explicitly subordinates general procurement procedures to specific statute. No re-numbering, repeal, or supersession. Minor correction to Stage 1's note that § 67-2806/2803 were 'unchanged in 2025-2026' - both were in fact amended by 2025 ch. 133 - but this does not affect the substantive finding that § 33-1510 controls.

challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: Some Idaho district interprets or applies § 33-1510 differently than others on contract term, rebid, or renewal mechanics; OR the SDE-approved mandatory model contract isn't actually mandatory; OR districts have been allowed to deviate by board policy.
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: Re-verify § 33-1510(1) verbatim mandatory-model-contract language; re-verify state-superintendent approval gateway (21-day review); review Mountain Home SD No. 193 - Caldwell Transportation Co. SY24-29 sample contract reference; verify SDE departmental page links to single statewide model contract; search for ISBA-policy or AG-opinion variance signals.
challengeMatrixRows.result: Confirmed. § 33-1510(1) verbatim mandates use of the 'current pupil transportation model contract developed by the state department of education' for ALL contracts entered into by boards of trustees. State-superintendent approval gateway (21 calendar days) is uniform. Sample district contract evidence (Mountain Home SD No. 193 - Caldwell Transportation; 5-year/single-renewal structure recited) is consistent with statewide framework. No AG opinion, ISBA policy, or auditor report flagging variance was located.

challengeMatrixRows.findingChallenged: Special Education = no separate term/rebid/procurement/renewal regime
challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: There is a separate Idaho statutory or administrative-code regime, or distinct SDE Special Education guidance, that creates a different contracting/procurement/term/renewal framework for IEP-mandated, McKinney-Vento, foster, homeless, alternative, or disability transportation contracts.
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: Full text extraction of SDE 'Transportation of Students with Disabilities' guidance (REVIEWED December 2024) - all 4 pages, 6 sections reviewed; review of 2025 ch. 278 (H 396) authorized-vehicle amendments to chapter 15; § 33-1510 text confirms 'All contracts entered into by boards of trustees for the transportation of pupils' (no carve-out); IDAPA 08.02.02 full PDF reviewed.
challengeMatrixRows.result: Confirmed. SDE Special Education guidance is entirely operational/IEP-team/FAPE-LRE/behavior - no procurement, contract-term, or renewal differentiation. § 33-1510 covers 'All contracts ... for the transportation of pupils' without carve-out. 2025 ch. 278 'authorized vehicles' amendments are operational/equipment-eligibility rules for which vehicles may carry special-needs, homeless, foster, and alternative-program students - not a separate procurement framework. Federal IDEA reaches only the duty to transport, not state procurement rules.

challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: § 33-1510 was amended, repealed, renumbered, or superseded after the 2022 ch. 87 amendment.
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: Direct re-fetch of § 33-1510 official page (verifies most-recent amendment text shows 2022 ch. 87); 2023, 2024, 2025, 2026 Code Sections Affected indices reviewed; 2025 H 396 (Ch. 278) full bill verified to amend §§ 33-1501, 33-1504, 33-1506, 33-1509, 33-1515 only; 2026 H 815 full bill verified to amend § 33-1006 only.
challengeMatrixRows.result: Confirmed. § 33-1510 has not been amended since 2022 ch. 87, § 1, p. 251. Code currency is to July 1 of each session year; the website-published version is current through the 2026 regular session as of 2026-06-23.

perFieldVerdicts.note: 5 years initial + 1 renewal of up to 5 years; § 33-1510(4) Clean School Bus federal-funding 10-year single-term exception. Verbatim text of § 33-1510(2) & (4) re-verified from official Idaho Legislature page; SDE Model Contract (June 2025) operationalizes identically. No grandfathering, multi-year, or annual-termination authority found.

Required at term-end except single conditional § 33-1510(2) renewal (disclosure-in-bid-notice prerequisite). § 67-2803 exclusions list (18 categories) does not exempt pupil transportation; § 67-2802 subordinates general procurement to transportation-specific § 33-1510.

One-time, up-to-5-year renewal after renegotiation with board approval; mandatory disclosure in original bid notice; state-superintendent contract review (21 calendar days, appealable to State Board of Education); CPI/fuel escalators permitted per SDE Model Contract; no automatic/evergreen rollover. All verbatim re-verified.

Idaho Code § 33-1510 (Title 33 ch. 15), last amended 2022 ch. 87, § 1, p. 251; supporting §§ 33-402(2), 33-601(2), 33-1511; Title 67 ch. 28 (§§ 67-2801, 2802, 2803, 2806) supplementary. Official URL re-fetched and verbatim text matches. Minor Stage-1 verification-note inaccuracy corrected: §§ 67-2803 and 67-2806 were amended by 2025 ch. 133 (Stage 1 said unchanged 2025-2026) - does not affect field finding.

No - mandatory use of SDE-developed model contract under § 33-1510(1); state-superintendent approval gateway produces uniform statewide application. No AG opinion, ISBA policy, or auditor report flagging variance located; sample district contracts (Mountain Home SD 193 / Caldwell Transportation Co.) recite § 33-1510(2) framework consistently.

No separate procurement/term/renewal regime. SDE 'Transportation of Students with Disabilities' guidance (REVIEWED December 2024) is entirely operational/IEP-team. § 33-1510 covers 'All contracts ... for the transportation of pupils' without carve-out. Federal IDEA reaches only the duty to transport, not state procurement.

verificationNotes:
**Adversarial method.** Stage 2 re-opened every Stage 1 citation on its official source, extracted load-bearing verbatim text, and ran disconfirming searches on each field per the brief.

**SOURCE-VALIDATION REVISIT (load-bearing primary citation - Idaho Code § 33-1510).** Re-opened https://legislature.idaho.gov/statutesrules/idstat/Title33/T33CH15/SECT33-1510/ via WebFetch on 2026-06-23. The page returned and the verbatim subsection (1), (2), (3), and (4) text matched Stage 1's quotations exactly: 5-year cap, single-time renewal-with-current-contractor (up to 5 years), renegotiation/board-determination requirement, disclosure-in-bid-notice prerequisite, lowest-responsible-bidder rule, twice-published notice cross-reference to § 33-402(2), and the § 33-1510(4) federal Clean School Bus 10-year exception citing 42 U.S.C. 16091. Last amendment notation: 2022 ch. 87, § 1, p. 251. Status: VERIFIED.

**Cross-referenced statutes re-verified.** § 33-402(2) - verbatim text re-confirmed, transportation-services 4-week notice rule intact, last amended 2011 ch. 151 § 15 (status: verified). § 33-601(2) - verbatim text re-confirmed, channels school-district contracts to Title 67 ch. 28 except curricular materials, last amended 2012 ch. 15 § 1 (status: verified). § 67-2802 - verbatim subordination-to-specific-statute language re-confirmed (status: verified). § 67-2803 - 18-category exclusion list reviewed; pupil transportation NOT among them; last amended 2025 ch. 133, § 1 (status: verified - minor Stage-1 verification-note correction needed; field finding unaffected). § 67-2806 - $100,000/$250,000 thresholds re-confirmed; last amended 2025 ch. 133, § 3 (status: verified - minor Stage-1 verification-note correction needed; field finding unaffected). § 33-1511 - state-board powers re-confirmed; last amended 2004 ch. 135 (status: verified).

**Official guidance re-verified.** SDE Model Contract for Transportation Services (June 2025): downloaded via WebFetch, extracted text from word/document.xml (92 paragraphs); verbatim Term-and-Renewal, Payment-Schedule (CPI/fuel escalator), Termination-for-Fiscal-Necessity (including the verbatim 'Nothing in this section shall be construed as ability by the District to terminate for its convenience'), and Termination-for-Cause clauses all matched Stage 1's quotations (status: verified). SDE 'Transportation of Students with Disabilities' guidance (REVIEWED December 2024): downloaded via WebFetch, full 4-page PDF text extracted via pypdf; all 6 sections (District Responsibility, IEP Team Decision, Transportation Representative, Determining Special Transportation Needs, Managing Behavior, Resources) confirmed operational only - no procurement, contracting, term, or renewal language (status: verified).

**Administrative-code re-verified.** IDAPA 08.02.02 - full 40-page PDF extracted via pypdf; §§ 150 (Transportation/Minimum Bus Construction Standards), 160 (Maintenance/Inspections), 170 (Drivers/Vehicle Operation), 180 (Written Policy), 190 (Program Operations) all reviewed; no contract-term, rebid, procurement, or renewal language. § 190's cross-reference to the Pupil Transportation Support Program's 'contracting for transportation services' is a reimbursement-eligibility/standards reference, not a procurement rule (status: verified negative finding).

**Currentness sweep.** 2025 ch. 278 (H 396) - 2025 Code Sections Affected page directly fetched; affected sections: 33-1501 amended, 33-1504 new + repealed, 33-1506 amended, 33-1509 amended, 33-1515 new section added. § 33-1510 NOT amended (status: verified). 2026 H 815 - full bill PDF fetched and extracted via pypdf; amends § 33-1006 (Transportation Support Program funding) only. § 33-1510 NOT amended (status: verified). 2026 Code Sections Affected index returned HTTP 404 (page not yet published); offset by direct H 815 review.

**Negative search paths verified.** Searched: longer-term/multi-year/successive/option/extension authority (none beyond § 33-1510(4) federal exception); sole-source/emergency/piggyback/cooperative/interlocal authority for pupil transportation (none; § 67-2803 exclusions list reviewed); automatic/evergreen/rollover renewal authority (none); separate special-education/IEP/McKinney-Vento/foster/homeless/alternative transportation procurement framework (none); district-variance interpretive divergence on term/rebid/renewal (none - mandatory model-contract regime + state-superintendent approval gateway produce uniformity); AG opinions specifically on § 33-1510 (none located).

**Conflict check.** No authoritative source conflicts the rule. § 33-1510 (transportation-specific) and Title 67 ch. 28 (general procurement) coexist; § 67-2802 expressly subordinates the general procedures to specific-statute provisions, and § 33-601(2) is the bridge that brings school-district contracts under Title 67 ch. 28 generally. § 33-402(2) provides the bidding-notice timing referenced by § 33-1510(3). No ambiguity about which controls.

**Final adversarial verdict.** All seven challenge rows resolved as 'Confirmed' on re-verified primary sources. Two minor Stage-1 verification-note inaccuracies identified and corrected herein: (a) § 67-2806 was amended by 2025 ch. 133, § 3 (not 'unchanged in 2025-2026'); (b) § 67-2803 was amended by 2025 ch. 133, § 1. Neither affects the substantive field findings - § 33-1510 controls as the transportation-specific statute. No re-research pass needed; no field downgraded.
